mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that dynamically manages DNS records for Kubernetes resources such as Services and Ingresses across various DNS providers. It is particularly useful for automating DNS management in cloud environments, allowing seamless integration of Kubernetes services with public DNS. Key trade-offs include the need for proper permissions on the DNS provider and potential latency in DNS updates.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the ExternalDNS Helm chart.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ExternalDNS in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Replace 'aws' with your DNS provider as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create an ExternalDNS Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for ExternalDNS to manage DNS records based on your Kubernetes resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: my-external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

```

> Make sure to replace 'example.com' with your actual domain.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: my-external-dns
spec:
  provider: aws
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step removes ExternalDNS from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all DNS records managed by ExternalDNS, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete customresourcedefinition externaldns.k8s.io

```

> This command removes the ExternalDNS release.
> This command deletes the ExternalDNS CRD.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade ExternalDNS to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get deployment external-dns -n kube-system -o yaml > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl rollout status deployment/external-dns -n kube-system

```

> Backup the current deployment configuration.
> This command upgrades ExternalDNS to the specified version.
> Verify that the deployment has been successfully upgraded.
commands: kubectl get deployment external-dns -n kube-system -o yaml > external-dns-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create record set'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates insufficient permissions for the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the IAM role or service account used by ExternalDNS has the necessary permissions to create DNS records.

troubleshooting.title: No DNS records created for services
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** ExternalDNS may not be correctly configured to watch the specified services or ingress resources.

**Fix:**
Check the ExternalDNS logs for errors and ensure that the correct annotations are set on your services or ingresses.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS pod is in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if ExternalDNS is not able to communicate with the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Verify the network connectivity and credentials for the DNS provider in the ExternalDNS configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved handling of multiple zones.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns' returning a status of 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
